narrative:
Dear Student,

I understand that receiving a rejection for your university application can be disheartening, and I want to help you understand the reasons behind this decision. The model used to evaluate your application predicted a 55.4% probability that you might not pass the bar exam on your first attempt. This prediction led to the rejection of your application, as the model aims to identify students who are likely to succeed on their first try.

Let's look at the factors that influenced this prediction. Your academic performance in Year 1 and Year 3, represented by your decile rankings, were the most significant factors. These rankings were lower than the average, which contributed to the model's prediction that you might struggle with the bar exam. Your LSAT score was slightly above average, which is a positive aspect, but it was not enough to outweigh the influence of your decile rankings.

Your family's income bracket and racial background also played a role. While your family income is in the middle bracket, which is typical, the model considered this alongside other factors. Being Hispanic, a minority group in the dataset, also influenced the prediction, although it was not the most critical factor. Your undergraduate GPA was higher than average, which is a strong point in your favor, but again, it was not enough to counterbalance the other factors.

In terms of influence, your decile rankings were the most impactful, followed by your LSAT score, family income, and racial background. Gender and whether you plan to study full-time or part-time had the least influence on the prediction.

To improve your chances of passing the bar exam in the future, you might focus on enhancing your academic performance in any ongoing or future studies. Consider seeking additional support or resources to strengthen your understanding of key subjects. Your strong undergraduate GPA and LSAT score are promising, so building on these strengths could help you succeed.

Remember, this prediction is not a definitive measure of your abilities or potential. With dedication and the right support, you can work towards achieving your goals.
